# MicroUI Presenter Guide

Launch a fullscreen, frameless presentation window with a built-in chat sidebar. You describe slides in the chat → the agent generates HTML → slides render in real-time on the left.

## Overview

Presenter mode is a single MicroUI window with a split layout:

- **Left (65%):** Slide area — renders HTML fragments generated by the agent
- **Right (35%):** Chat sidebar — sends prompts to the responses API with an injected system prompt that instructs the agent to return `[SLIDE]...[/SLIDE]` markers

The agent never calls tools from chat. Instead, the JavaScript client-side parses the streamed response, extracts slide HTML from markers, and renders it directly.

## How It Works

```
User types in chat sidebar
  │
  ▼
fetch() POST → http://127.0.0.1:15210/v1/responses
  │
  ├── System prompt injected: "wrap slide HTML in [SLIDE]...[/SLIDE]"
  │
  ▼
Agent responds with:
  [SLIDE]<div style="...">slide content</div>[/SLIDE]
  Here's your slide! 🎨
  │
  ▼
JavaScript regex extracts:
  ├── match[1] → slideArea.innerHTML (left panel)
  └── remaining text → chat bubble (right panel)
```

## Prerequisites

- Responses extension running (`responses_restart` if needed)
- MicroUI binary built and available

## How to Launch

Use `microui_show` with the presenter HTML, frameless, and fullscreen:

```
microui_show:
  name: presenter
  frameless: true
  fullscreen: true
  html: <presenter HTML — see Template section below>
```

Key flags:
- `frameless: true` — no title bar, edge-to-edge content
- `fullscreen: true` — true OS fullscreen (covers taskbar)
- Include an **Exit** link in the footer since there's no close button

## Template

The presenter HTML has three main sections:

### 1. Layout Structure

```html
<body>
  <div class="pres">        <!-- Left: slide area -->
    <div class="pres-area">
      <div class="slide-content" id="slideArea">
        <!-- Slides render here -->
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="pres-footer">
      <span id="slideNum">Slide 1</span>
      <span class="badge">● Live</span>
      <a class="exit" onclick="window.genesis.close()">✕ Exit</a>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="chat">        <!-- Right: chat sidebar -->
    <div class="chat-header">...</div>
    <div class="messages" id="messages">...</div>
    <div class="input-area">
      <textarea id="input" placeholder="Describe a slide..."></textarea>
      <button onclick="sendMessage()">Send</button>
    </div>
  </div>
</body>
```

### 2. System Prompt (injected into every chat message)

The system prompt tells the agent how to format responses:

```js
var SYSTEM_PROMPT = 'You are in PRESENTATION MODE. ' +
  'When the user asks for a slide, respond with the slide HTML wrapped in ' +
  '[SLIDE]...[/SLIDE] markers. The HTML inside should be a self-contained ' +
  'fragment with inline styles (dark theme, bg #0d1117, text #e6edf3). ' +
  'Use large fonts, vibrant colors (#58a6ff, #3fb950, #d29922, #f0883e, #bc4dff). ' +
  'After the [SLIDE] block, add a brief chat message.';
```

### 3. SSE Stream Parsing

The chat sends to the responses API and reads the SSE stream:

```js
fetch('http://127.0.0.1:' + API_PORT + '/v1/responses', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    model: 'copilot',
    input: SYSTEM_PROMPT + ' User request: ' + text,
    stream: true
  })
})
```

SSE events use named event types:

```text
event: response.output_text.delta
data: {"delta":"token text"}
```

Parse with a pump loop reading `event:` headers, filtering for `response.output_text.delta`, and accumulating `data.delta` tokens.

### 4. Slide Extraction (on stream done)

```js
var match = fullResponse.match(/\[SLIDE\]([\s\S]*?)\[\/SLIDE\]/);
if (match) {
  slideArea.innerHTML = match[1];        // render slide
  slideNum.textContent = 'Slide ' + (++slideCount);
  var chatText = fullResponse
    .replace(/\[SLIDE\][\s\S]*?\[\/SLIDE\]/g, '')
    .trim();
  // Show chatText in chat bubble
}
```

## Slide Design Guidelines

Slides should be self-contained HTML fragments with inline styles:

- **Background:** `#0d1117` (matches presenter dark theme)
- **Text:** `#e6edf3` (primary), `#8b949e` (secondary)
- **Accent colors:** `#58a6ff` (blue), `#3fb950` (green), `#d29922` (gold), `#f0883e` (orange), `#bc4dff` (purple)
- **Layout:** Use flexbox, center content, large fonts (1.5rem+)
- **Visual elements:** Emoji, bordered boxes, gradient backgrounds, arrows for flow diagrams

Example slide:

```html
[SLIDE]
<div style="display:flex;flex-direction:column;align-items:center;padding:40px">
  <h1 style="font-size:2.4rem;color:#58a6ff;margin-bottom:40px">🏗️ Title</h1>
  <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:16px">
    <div style="background:rgba(31,111,235,0.15);border:2px solid #1f6feb;
                border-radius:12px;padding:20px 24px;text-align:center">
      <div style="font-size:1.8rem">👤</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.95rem;font-weight:600;color:#58a6ff">Label</div>
    </div>
    <div style="font-size:2rem;color:#30363d">→</div>
    <!-- more boxes... -->
  </div>
</div>
[/SLIDE]
```

## Architecture Notes

- **No tool calls from chat:** The responses API returns text only — it cannot trigger `microui_update` or other tools. All rendering is client-side JavaScript.
- **Model:** Use `model: 'copilot'` to route through the local responses API to the current agent session.
- **Streaming:** Tokens arrive via SSE and are accumulated. Slide extraction happens on stream completion (`onDone`), not incrementally.
- **No persistence:** Closing the window loses all slides and chat history.

## Limitations

- **One slide at a time** — new slides replace the previous one (no deck navigation yet)
- **No slide export** — slides exist only in the DOM
- **Responses API required** — must be running on port 15210
- **Frameless requires exit button** — include a `window.genesis.close()` link in the footer

## Troubleshooting

- **"(no response)" in chat:** Ensure the responses extension is running (`responses_restart`)
- **Slide doesn't render:** Check that the agent response includes `[SLIDE]...[/SLIDE]` markers
- **Window not fullscreen:** Use `fullscreen: true` (not just large width/height)
- **Scrollbar visible:** Ensure the HTML has `margin:0;padding:0` on body — the wrapper template handles this automatically
